source.purpose: Evidence-backed real-corpus Type-4 frontier items used to raise practical semantic completeness.

entry_contract.case_id: Stable id for this real-corpus frontier item.
entry_contract.candidate_axis: Prioritizer candidate or semantic axis, such as membership_contains.
entry_contract.language: Primary source language for the audited span.
entry_contract.locations: One or more repo-relative source spans participating in the claim.
entry_contract.semantic_claim: Concrete equivalence or non-equivalence claim being audited.
entry_contract.evidence: Same-spec/property evidence for positives, or counterexample/non-equivalence witness for negatives.
entry_contract.detector: Baseline and candidate detection status for the audited spans.
entry_contract.proof_invariant: The narrow proof fact required before the detector may merge the item.
entry_contract.hard_negative_siblings: Adjacent boundary cases that must remain unmerged.
entry_contract.batch: Batch id once the item is selected for implementation.
entry_contract.notes: Short audit notes, including why unsupported items are not detector work.

items.semantic_claim: The two EnumSet membership predicates share the same item set and searched element when item order is ignored.
items.evidence: IL confirms both predicates lower to EnumSet.of(...).contains(unit), but the second enclosing method has an additional HALF_DAYS exclusion assertion, so the method-level units are not an evidence-backed Type-4 clone pair.

detector.baseline_result: No family contains testWithEnumSourceInclude and testWithEnumSourceRangeExclude; selected before/after EnumSet whitelist experiment also produced no new real family.

items.proof_invariant: Would require only unshadowed java.util.EnumSet.of with strict-safe arguments and order-insensitive membership collection canon, but this is not sufficient to close a real method-level miss here.
items.notes: Do not implement as a completeness batch unless another real corpus counterpart is found.

items.semantic_claim: For a proven Java array receiver, Arrays.asList(array).contains(value) is membership in the array elements.
items.evidence: The source declares skipTargets as String[], but current IL only preserves a var/field use and not an array type fact at the contains call.

detector.baseline_result: IL shows Arrays.asList(skipTargets).contains(targetName) without a retained array proof fact.

items.proof_invariant: Requires a frontend proof fact that the single Arrays.asList argument is a Java array, plus mutation/alias boundaries for field or parameter provenance.
hard_negative_siblings: Arrays.asList(list).contains(value), which tests whether value equals the list object
mutated or reassigned field provenance for skipTargets
items.notes: Do not treat generic Arrays.asList(x) as collection-param membership without an array proof.

items.semantic_claim: The two supportsParameter implementations are syntactically and behaviorally identical for the documented ParameterResolver shape.
items.evidence: near/syntax scan reports a real duplicate block, but semantic scan alone reports no family because the expression depends on a dynamic JUnit API call chain.

detector.baseline_command: nose scan HttpServerExtension.java HttpTests.java --mode semantic --format json --top 0 --min-lines 1 --min-tokens 1
detector.baseline_result: 0 semantic families; syntax,semantic reports a block family.

items.proof_invariant: Would require proving Class literal equality and the purity/identity of parameterContext.getParameter().getType(), which is a library-specific receiver chain not currently modeled.
hard_negative_siblings: calls with side effects or mutation before getType
items.notes: Not suitable for a soundness-preserving Type-4 detector batch without shared proof facts for Java reflection/JUnit APIs.
